content.content:
1	package broadcast
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"sync"
6	
7		pb "github.com/nginx/agent/v3/api/grpc/mpi/v1"
8		"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/uuid"
9	)
10	
11	//go:generate go tool counterfeiter -generate
12	
13	//counterfeiter:generate . Broadcaster
14	
15	// Broadcaster defines an interface for consumers to subscribe to File updates.
16	type Broadcaster interface {
17		Subscribe() SubscriberChannels
18		Send(NginxAgentMessage) bool
19		CancelSubscription(string)
20	}
21	
22	// SubscriberChannels are the channels sent to the subscriber to listen and respond on.
23	// The ID is used for map lookup to delete a subscriber when it's gone.
24	type SubscriberChannels struct {
25		ListenCh   <-chan NginxAgentMessage
26		ResponseCh chan<- struct{}
27		ID         string
28	}
29	
30	// storedChannels are the same channels used in the SubscriberChannels, but reverse direction.
31	// These are used to store the channels for the broadcaster to send and listen on,
32	// and can be looked up in the map using the same ID.
33	type storedChannels struct {
34		listenCh chan<- NginxAgentMessage
35		// responseCh is only received from by the broadcaster; subscribers send on it to signal completion.
36		responseCh <-chan struct{}
37		// listenerCtx is used to unblock the publisher if a listener unsubscribes or if the broadcaster is shutting
38		// down. It is a child of the broadcaster context, so it will also be canceled on shutdown.
39		listenerCtx context.Context
40		cancel      context.CancelFunc
41		id          string
42	}
43	
44	// DeploymentBroadcaster sends out a signal when an nginx Deployment has updated
45	// configuration files. The signal is received by any agent Subscription that cares
46	// about this Deployment. The agent Subscription will then send a response of whether or not
47	// the configuration was successfully applied.
48	type DeploymentBroadcaster struct {
49		publishCh chan NginxAgentMessage
50		subCh     chan storedChannels
51		unsubCh   chan string
52		listeners map[string]storedChannels
53		doneCh    chan struct{}
54		// broadcasterCtx is the main context for the broadcaster, which is canceled on shutdown.
55		// It is the parent context for all listener contexts.
56		broadcasterCtx    context.Context
57		broadcasterCancel context.CancelFunc
58		// mu protects the listeners map. It is needed for concurrent access to
59		// the listeners map in the publisher and subscriber goroutines.
60		mu sync.RWMutex
61	}
62	
63	// NewDeploymentBroadcaster returns a new instance of a DeploymentBroadcaster.
64	func NewDeploymentBroadcaster(ctx context.Context) *DeploymentBroadcaster {
65		broadcasterCtx, broadcasterCancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
66	
67		broadcaster := &DeploymentBroadcaster{
68			listeners:         make(map[string]storedChannels),
69			publishCh:         make(chan NginxAgentMessage),
70			subCh:             make(chan storedChannels),
71			unsubCh:           make(chan string),
72			doneCh:            make(chan struct{}),
73			broadcasterCtx:    broadcasterCtx,
74			broadcasterCancel: broadcasterCancel,
75		}
76	
77		go broadcaster.subscriber()
78		go broadcaster.publisher()
79	
80		return broadcaster
81	}
82	
83	// Subscribe allows a listener to subscribe to broadcast messages. It returns the channel
84	// to listen on for messages, as well as a channel to respond on.
85	func (b *DeploymentBroadcaster) Subscribe() SubscriberChannels {
86		listenCh := make(chan NginxAgentMessage)
87		responseCh := make(chan struct{})
88		id := string(uuid.NewUUID())
89		// Create listener context as child of broadcaster context
90	
91		listenerCtx, cancel := context.WithCancel(b.broadcasterCtx)
92	
93		subscriberChans := SubscriberChannels{
94			ID:         id,
95			ListenCh:   listenCh,
96			ResponseCh: responseCh,
97		}
98		storedChans := storedChannels{
99			id:          id,
100			listenCh:    listenCh,
101			responseCh:  responseCh,
102			listenerCtx: listenerCtx,
103			cancel:      cancel,
104		}
105	
106		select {
107		case <-b.broadcasterCtx.Done():
108			// Broadcaster is shutting down or already shut down. Returns channels so the caller
109			// can still interact with them while shutting down.
110			return subscriberChans
111		case b.subCh <- storedChans:
112			// Subscription sent successfully
113		}
114	
115		return subscriberChans
116	}
117	
118	// Send the message to all listeners. Wait for all listeners to respond.
119	// Returns true if there were listeners that received and responded to the message.
120	func (b *DeploymentBroadcaster) Send(message NginxAgentMessage) bool {
121		// Try to send message, but can be interrupted by shutdown
122		select {
123		case b.publishCh <- message:
124		case <-b.broadcasterCtx.Done():
125			return false
126		}
127	
128		// Wait for completion, but can be interrupted by shutdown
129		select {
130		case <-b.doneCh:
131		case <-b.broadcasterCtx.Done():
132			return false
133		}
134	
135		b.mu.RLock()
136		defer b.mu.RUnlock()
137	
138		return len(b.listeners) > 0
139	}
140	
141	// CancelSubscription removes a Subscriber from the channel list.
142	func (b *DeploymentBroadcaster) CancelSubscription(id string) {
143		select {
144		case b.unsubCh <- id:
145		case <-b.broadcasterCtx.Done():
146			// Broadcaster is shutting down or already shut down; avoid blocking send.
147			return
148		}
149	}
150	
151	// subscriber handles subscription management and stop conditions. It is responsible for cleaning up resources
152	// on shutdown/function return, specifically by canceling the broadcaster context (and thus all listener
153	// contexts) to unblock any pending publisher goroutines.
154	func (b *DeploymentBroadcaster) subscriber() {
155		// Canceling the broadcaster context will cancel all listener contexts since they are children,
156		// which will unblock any publishers waiting on those contexts.
157		defer b.broadcasterCancel()
158	
159		for {
160			select {
161			case <-b.broadcasterCtx.Done():
162				return
163			case channels := <-b.subCh:
164				b.mu.Lock()
165				b.listeners[channels.id] = channels
166				b.mu.Unlock()
167			case id := <-b.unsubCh:
168				b.mu.Lock()
169				if channels, exists := b.listeners[id]; exists {
170					// Cancel listener's context to unblock publisher
171					channels.cancel()
172					delete(b.listeners, id)
173				}
174				b.mu.Unlock()
175			}
176		}
177	}
178	
179	// publisher handles message publishing.
180	func (b *DeploymentBroadcaster) publisher() {
181		// Due to the split between the subscription management and publishing,
182		// every blocking select in this function needs a way to be unblocked
183		// by the subscriber function.
184		for {
185			select {
186			case <-b.broadcasterCtx.Done():
187				return
188			case msg := <-b.publishCh:
189				b.mu.RLock()
190				currentListeners := make(map[string]storedChannels, len(b.listeners))
191				for k, v := range b.listeners {
192					currentListeners[k] = v
193				}
194				b.mu.RUnlock()
195	
196				// Send to all listeners
197				var wg sync.WaitGroup
198				for _, channels := range currentListeners {
199					wg.Go(func() {
200						select {
201						case <-channels.listenerCtx.Done():
202							return
203						case <-b.broadcasterCtx.Done():
204							return
205						case channels.listenCh <- msg:
206							// Message sent successfully, now wait for response in next select
207						}
208	
209						select {
210						case <-channels.listenerCtx.Done():
211							return
212						case <-b.broadcasterCtx.Done():
213							return
214						case <-channels.responseCh:
215							// Response received, continue
216							return
217						}
218					})
219				}
220				wg.Wait()
221	
222				select {
223				// If the broadcaster context is done, there may be nothing to receive
224				// the done signal, so we return to avoid blocking.
225				case <-b.broadcasterCtx.Done():
226					return
227				case b.doneCh <- struct{}{}:
228					// Signal that publishing is done and all responses received
229				}
230			}
231		}
232	}
233	
234	// MessageType is the type of message to be sent.
235	type MessageType int
236	
237	const (
238		// ConfigApplyRequest sends files to update nginx configuration.
239		ConfigApplyRequest MessageType = iota
240		// APIRequest sends an NGINX Plus API request to update configuration.
241		APIRequest
242	)
243	
244	// NginxAgentMessage is sent to all subscribers to send to the nginx agents for either a ConfigApplyRequest
245	// or an APIActionRequest.
246	type NginxAgentMessage struct {
247		// ConfigVersion is the hashed configuration version of the included files.
248		ConfigVersion string
249		// NGINXPlusAction is an NGINX Plus API action to be sent.
250		NGINXPlusAction *pb.NGINXPlusAction
251		// FileOverviews contain the overviews of all files to be sent.
252		FileOverviews []*pb.File
253		// Type defines the type of message to be sent.
254		Type MessageType
255	}
256

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"context"
6		"math"
7	
8		"github.com/go-logr/logr"
9		pb "github.com/nginx/agent/v3/api/grpc/mpi/v1"
10		"github.com/nginx/agent/v3/pkg/files"
11		"google.golang.org/grpc"
12		"google.golang.org/grpc/codes"
13		"google.golang.org/grpc/status"
14	
15		agentgrpc "github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/v2/internal/controller/nginx/agent/grpc"
16		grpcContext "github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/v2/internal/controller/nginx/agent/grpc/context"
17	)
18	
19	const defaultChunkSize uint32 = 2097152 // 2MB
20	
21	// File is an nginx configuration file that the nginx agent gets from the control plane
22	// after a ConfigApplyRequest.
23	type File struct {
24		Meta     *pb.FileMeta
25		Contents []byte
26	}
27	
28	// fileService handles file management between the control plane and the agent.
29	type fileService struct {
30		pb.FileServiceServer
31		nginxDeployments *DeploymentStore
32		connTracker      agentgrpc.ConnectionsTracker
33		logger           logr.Logger
34	}
35	
36	func newFileService(
37		logger logr.Logger,
38		depStore *DeploymentStore,
39		connTracker agentgrpc.ConnectionsTracker,
40	) *fileService {
41		return &fileService{
42			logger:           logger,
43			nginxDeployments: depStore,
44			connTracker:      connTracker,
45		}
46	}
47	
48	func (fs *fileService) Register(server *grpc.Server) {
49		pb.RegisterFileServiceServer(server, fs)
50	}
51	
52	// GetFile is called by the agent when it needs to download a file for a ConfigApplyRequest.
53	// The deployment object used to get the files is already LOCKED when this function is called,
54	// before the ConfigApply transaction is started.
55	func (fs *fileService) GetFile(
56		ctx context.Context,
57		req *pb.GetFileRequest,
58	) (*pb.GetFileResponse, error) {
59		grpcInfo, ok := grpcContext.FromContext(ctx)
60		if !ok {
61			return nil, agentgrpc.ErrStatusInvalidConnection
62		}
63	
64		if req.GetFileMeta() == nil {
65			return nil, status.Error(codes.InvalidArgument, "invalid request")
66		}
67	
68		contents, err := fs.getFileContents(req, grpcInfo.UUID)
69		if err != nil {
70			return nil, err
71		}
72	
73		return &pb.GetFileResponse{
74			Contents: &pb.FileContents{
75				Contents: contents,
76			},
77		}, nil
78	}
79	
80	// GetFileStream is called by the agent when it needs to download a file in chunks for a ConfigApplyRequest.
81	// The deployment object used to get the files is already LOCKED when this function is called,
82	// before the ConfigApply transaction is started.
83	func (fs *fileService) GetFileStream(
84		req *pb.GetFileRequest,
85		server grpc.ServerStreamingServer[pb.FileDataChunk],
86	) error {
87		grpcInfo, ok := grpcContext.FromContext(server.Context())
88		if !ok {
89			return agentgrpc.ErrStatusInvalidConnection
90		}
91	
92		if req.GetFileMeta() == nil || req.GetMessageMeta() == nil {
93			return status.Error(codes.InvalidArgument, "invalid request")
94		}
95	
96		contents, err := fs.getFileContents(req, grpcInfo.UUID)
97		if err != nil {
98			return err
99		}
100	
101		size := req.GetFileMeta().GetSize()
102		var sizeUint32 uint32
103		if size > math.MaxUint32 {
104			return status.Error(codes.Internal, "file size is too large and cannot be converted to uint32")
105		}
106		sizeUint32 = uint32(size) //nolint:gosec // validation check performed on previous line
107		hash := req.GetFileMeta().GetHash()
108	
109		fs.logger.V(1).Info(
110			"Sending chunked file to agent",
111			"file", req.GetFileMeta().GetName(),
112			"correlation_id", req.GetMessageMeta().GetCorrelationId(),
113		)
114	
115		if err := files.SendChunkedFile(
116			req.GetMessageMeta(),
117			pb.FileDataChunk_Header{
118				Header: &pb.FileDataChunkHeader{
119					ChunkSize: defaultChunkSize,
120					Chunks:    calculateChunks(sizeUint32, defaultChunkSize),
121					FileMeta: &pb.FileMeta{
122						Name:        req.GetFileMeta().GetName(),
123						Hash:        hash,
124						Permissions: req.GetFileMeta().GetPermissions(),
125						Size:        size,
126					},
127				},
128			},
129			bytes.NewReader(contents),
130			server,
131		); err != nil {
132			return status.Error(codes.Aborted, err.Error())
133		}
134	
135		return nil
136	}
137	
138	func (fs *fileService) getFileContents(req *pb.GetFileRequest, connKey string) ([]byte, error) {
139		conn := fs.connTracker.GetConnection(connKey)
140		if conn.InstanceID == "" {
141			return nil, status.Errorf(codes.Internal, "connection not found")
142		}
143	
144		deployment := fs.nginxDeployments.Get(conn.ParentName)
145		if deployment == nil {
146			return nil, status.Errorf(codes.Internal, "deployment not found in store")
147		}
148	
149		filename := req.GetFileMeta().GetName()
150		contents, fileFoundHash := deployment.GetFile(filename, req.GetFileMeta().GetHash())
151		if len(contents) == 0 {
152			fs.logger.V(1).Info(
153				"Error getting file for agent",
154				"file", filename,
155				"correlation_id", req.GetMessageMeta().GetCorrelationId(),
156			)
157			if fileFoundHash != "" {
158				fs.logger.V(1).Info(
159					"File found had wrong hash",
160					"hash_wanted", req.GetFileMeta().GetHash(),
161					"hash_found", fileFoundHash,
162					"correlation_id", req.GetMessageMeta().GetCorrelationId(),
163				)
164			}
165			return nil, status.Errorf(codes.NotFound, "file not found")
166		}
167	
168		fs.logger.V(1).Info(
169			"Getting file for agent",
170			"file", filename,
171			"file_hash", fileFoundHash,
172			"correlation_id", req.GetMessageMeta().GetCorrelationId(),
173		)
174	
175		return contents, nil
176	}
177	
178	func calculateChunks(fileSize uint32, chunkSize uint32) uint32 {
179		remainder, divide := fileSize%chunkSize, fileSize/chunkSize
180		if remainder > 0 {
181			return divide + 1
182		}
183		// if fileSize is divisible by chunkSize without remainder
184		// then we don't need the extra chunk for the remainder
185		return divide
186	}
187	
188	// GetOverview gets the overview of files for a particular configuration version of an instance.
189	// At the moment it doesn't appear to be used by the agent.
190	func (*fileService) GetOverview(context.Context, *pb.GetOverviewRequest) (*pb.GetOverviewResponse, error) {
191		return &pb.GetOverviewResponse{}, nil
192	}
193	
194	// UpdateOverview is called by agent on startup and whenever any files change on the instance.
195	// Since directly changing nginx configuration on the instance is not supported, NGF will send back an empty response.
196	// However, we do use this call to gather the list of referenced files in the nginx configuration in order to
197	// mark user mounted files as unmanaged so the agent does not attempt to modify them.
198	func (fs *fileService) UpdateOverview(
199		ctx context.Context,
200		req *pb.UpdateOverviewRequest,
201	) (*pb.UpdateOverviewResponse, error) {
202		grpcInfo, ok := grpcContext.FromContext(ctx)
203		if !ok {
204			return &pb.UpdateOverviewResponse{}, agentgrpc.ErrStatusInvalidConnection
205		}
206	
207		conn := fs.connTracker.GetConnection(grpcInfo.UUID)
208		if conn.InstanceID == "" {
209			return &pb.UpdateOverviewResponse{}, status.Errorf(codes.Internal, "connection not found")
210		}
211	
212		deployment := fs.nginxDeployments.Get(conn.ParentName)
213		if deployment == nil {
214			return &pb.UpdateOverviewResponse{}, status.Errorf(codes.Internal, "deployment not found in store")
215		}
216	
217		requestFiles := req.GetOverview().GetFiles()
218	
219		fileNames := make([]string, 0, len(requestFiles))
220		for _, f := range requestFiles {
221			fileNames = append(fileNames, f.GetFileMeta().GetName())
222		}
223	
224		deployment.FileLock.Lock()
225		deployment.latestFileNames = fileNames
226		deployment.FileLock.Unlock()
227	
228		return &pb.UpdateOverviewResponse{}, nil
229	}
230	
231	// UpdateFile is called by agent whenever any files change on the instance.
232	// Since directly changing nginx configuration on the instance is not supported, this is a no-op for NGF.
233	func (*fileService) UpdateFile(context.Context, *pb.UpdateFileRequest) (*pb.UpdateFileResponse, error) {
234		return &pb.UpdateFileResponse{}, nil
235	}
236	
237	// UpdateFileStream is called by agent whenever any files change on the instance.
238	// Since directly changing nginx configuration on the instance is not supported, this is a no-op for NGF.
239	func (*fileService) UpdateFileStream(grpc.ClientStreamingServer[pb.FileDataChunk, pb.UpdateFileResponse]) error {
240		return nil
241	}

6		"fmt"

12		v1 "k8s.io/api/core/v1"
13		"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types"

16		agentgrpc "github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/v2/internal/controller/nginx/agent/grpc"
17	)

19	// ignoreFiles is a list of static or base files that live in the
20	// nginx container that should not be touched by the agent. Any files
21	// that we add directly into the container should be added here.
22	var ignoreFiles = []string{
23		"/etc/nginx/nginx.conf",
24		"/etc/nginx/mime.types",
25		"/etc/nginx/grpc-error-locations.conf",
26		"/etc/nginx/grpc-error-pages.conf",
27		"/usr/share/nginx/html/50x.html",
28		"/usr/share/nginx/html/dashboard.html",
29		"/usr/share/nginx/html/index.html",
30		"/usr/share/nginx/html/nginx-modules-reference.pdf",
31	}

33	const fileMode = "0644"
34	
35	// Deployment represents an nginx Deployment. It contains its own nginx configuration files,
36	// a broadcaster for sending those files to all of its pods that are subscribed, and errors
37	// that may have occurred while applying configuration.
38	type Deployment struct {
39		// podStatuses is a map of all Pods for this Deployment and the most recent error
40		// (or nil if successful) that occurred on a config call to the nginx agent.
41		podStatuses map[string]error
42	
43		broadcaster broadcast.Broadcaster
44	
45		// gatewayName is the name of the Gateway associated with this Deployment.
46		gatewayName string
47	
48		imageVersion string
49	
50		configVersion string
51		// error that is set if a ConfigApply call failed for a Pod. This is needed
52		// because if subsequent upstream API calls are made within the same update event,
53		// and are successful, the previous error would be lost in the podStatuses map.
54		// It's used to preserve the error for when we write status after fully updating nginx.
55		latestConfigError error
56		// error that is set when at least one upstream API call failed for a Pod.
57		// This is needed because subsequent API calls within the same update event could succeed,
58		// and therefore the previous error would be lost in the podStatuses map. It's used to preserve
59		// the error for when we write status after fully updating nginx.
60		latestUpstreamError error
61	
62		nginxPlusActions []*pb.NGINXPlusAction
63		fileOverviews    []*pb.File
64		files            []File
65	
66		latestFileNames []string
67		volumeMounts    []v1.VolumeMount
68	
69		FileLock sync.RWMutex
70		errLock  sync.RWMutex
71	}
72	
73	// newDeployment returns a new Deployment object.
74	func newDeployment(broadcaster broadcast.Broadcaster, gatewayName string) *Deployment {
75		return &Deployment{
76			broadcaster: broadcaster,
77			podStatuses: make(map[string]error),
78			gatewayName: gatewayName,
79		}
80	}
81	
82	// GetGatewayName returns the name of the Gateway associated with this deployment.
83	func (d *Deployment) GetGatewayName() string {
84		return d.gatewayName
85	}
86	
87	// GetBroadcaster returns the deployment's broadcaster.
88	func (d *Deployment) GetBroadcaster() broadcast.Broadcaster {
89		return d.broadcaster
90	}
91	
92	// SetImageVersion sets the deployment's image version.
93	func (d *Deployment) SetImageVersion(imageVersion string) {
94		d.FileLock.Lock()
95		defer d.FileLock.Unlock()
96	
97		d.imageVersion = imageVersion
98	}
99	
100	// SetLatestConfigError sets the latest config apply error for the deployment.
101	func (d *Deployment) SetLatestConfigError(err error) {
102		d.errLock.Lock()
103		defer d.errLock.Unlock()
104	
105		d.latestConfigError = err
106	}
107	
108	// SetLatestUpstreamError sets the latest upstream update error for the deployment.
109	func (d *Deployment) SetLatestUpstreamError(err error) {
110		d.errLock.Lock()
111		defer d.errLock.Unlock()
112	
113		d.latestUpstreamError = err
114	}
115	
116	// GetLatestConfigError gets the latest config apply error for the deployment.
117	func (d *Deployment) GetLatestConfigError() error {
118		d.errLock.RLock()
119		defer d.errLock.RUnlock()
120	
121		return d.latestConfigError
122	}
123	
124	// GetLatestUpstreamError gets the latest upstream update error for the deployment.
125	func (d *Deployment) GetLatestUpstreamError() error {
126		d.errLock.RLock()
127		defer d.errLock.RUnlock()
128	
129		return d.latestUpstreamError
130	}
131	
132	// SetPodErrorStatus sets the error status of a Pod in this Deployment if applying the config failed.
133	func (d *Deployment) SetPodErrorStatus(pod string, err error) {
134		d.errLock.Lock()
135		defer d.errLock.Unlock()
136	
137		d.podStatuses[pod] = err
138	}
139	
140	// RemovePodStatus deletes a pod from the pod status map.
141	func (d *Deployment) RemovePodStatus(podName string) {
142		d.errLock.Lock()
143		defer d.errLock.Unlock()
144	
145		delete(d.podStatuses, podName)
146	}
147	
148	// GetConfigurationStatus returns the current config status for this Deployment. It combines
149	// the most recent errors (if they exist) for all Pods in the Deployment into a single error.
150	func (d *Deployment) GetConfigurationStatus() error {
151		d.errLock.RLock()
152		defer d.errLock.RUnlock()
153	
154		errs := make([]error, 0, len(d.podStatuses))
155		for _, err := range d.podStatuses {
156			errs = append(errs, err)
157		}
158	
159		if len(errs) == 1 {
160			return errs[0]
161		}
162	
163		return errors.Join(errs...)
164	}

166	/*
167	The following functions for the Deployment object are UNLOCKED, meaning that they are unsafe.
168	Callers of these functions MUST ensure the FileLock is set before calling.
169	
170	These functions are called as part of the ConfigApply or APIRequest processes. These entire processes
171	are locked by the caller, hence why the functions themselves do not set the locks.
172	*/
173	
174	// GetFileOverviews returns the current list of fileOverviews and configVersion for the deployment.
175	// The deployment FileLock MUST already be locked before calling this function.
176	func (d *Deployment) GetFileOverviews() ([]*pb.File, string) {
177		return d.fileOverviews, d.configVersion
178	}
179	
180	// GetNGINXPlusActions returns the current NGINX Plus API Actions for the deployment.
181	// The deployment FileLock MUST already be locked before calling this function.
182	func (d *Deployment) GetNGINXPlusActions() []*pb.NGINXPlusAction {
183		return d.nginxPlusActions
184	}
185	
186	// GetFile gets the requested file for the deployment and returns its contents.
187	// The deployment FileLock MUST already be locked before calling this function.
188	func (d *Deployment) GetFile(name, hash string) ([]byte, string) {
189		var fileFoundHash string
190		for _, file := range d.files {
191			if name == file.Meta.GetName() {
192				fileFoundHash = file.Meta.GetHash()
193				if hash == file.Meta.GetHash() {
194					return file.Contents, file.Meta.GetHash()
195				}
196			}
197		}
198	
199		return nil, fileFoundHash
200	}
201	
202	// SetFiles updates the nginx files and fileOverviews for the deployment and returns the message to send.
203	// The deployment FileLock MUST already be locked before calling this function.
204	func (d *Deployment) SetFiles(files []File, volumeMounts []v1.VolumeMount) *broadcast.NginxAgentMessage {
205		d.files = files
206		d.volumeMounts = volumeMounts
207	
208		return d.rebuildFileOverviews()
209	}
210	
211	// SetNGINXPlusActions updates the deployment's latest NGINX Plus Actions to perform if using NGINX Plus.
212	// Used by a Subscriber when it first connects.
213	// The deployment FileLock MUST already be locked before calling this function.
214	func (d *Deployment) SetNGINXPlusActions(actions []*pb.NGINXPlusAction) {
215		d.nginxPlusActions = actions
216	}

218	// UpdateWAFBundle replaces or inserts a WAF bundle file in the deployment's file list.
219	// It finds an existing file by its full path and replaces its contents and metadata,
220	// or appends a new file entry if the bundle does not yet exist.
221	// Returns a broadcast message if the config version changed (i.e. the bundle contents differ
222	// from what was previously stored), or nil if nothing changed.
223	// The deployment FileLock MUST already be locked before calling this function.
224	func (d *Deployment) UpdateWAFBundle(bundlePath string, data []byte) *broadcast.NginxAgentMessage {
225		newHash := filesHelper.GenerateHash(data)
226		newMeta := &pb.FileMeta{
227			Name:        bundlePath,
228			Hash:        newHash,
229			Permissions: fileMode,
230			Size:        int64(len(data)),
231		}
232	
233		found := false
234		for i, f := range d.files {
235			if f.Meta.GetName() == bundlePath {
236				d.files[i] = File{
237					Meta:     newMeta,
238					Contents: data,
239				}
240				found = true
241				break
242			}
243		}
244	
245		if !found {
246			d.files = append(d.files, File{
247				Meta:     newMeta,
248				Contents: data,
249			})
250		}
251	
252		return d.rebuildFileOverviews()
253	}
254	
255	// RemoveWAFBundle removes a WAF bundle file from the deployment's file list.
256	// Returns a broadcast message if the config version changed, or nil if the bundle
257	// was not found or nothing changed.
258	// The deployment FileLock MUST already be locked before calling this function.
259	func (d *Deployment) RemoveWAFBundle(bundlePath string) *broadcast.NginxAgentMessage {
260		for i, f := range d.files {
261			if f.Meta.GetName() == bundlePath {
262				d.files = append(d.files[:i], d.files[i+1:]...)
263				return d.rebuildFileOverviews()
264			}
265		}
266		return nil
267	}
268	
269	// rebuildFileOverviews regenerates the file overviews and config version from the current
270	// file list. Returns a broadcast message if the config version changed.
271	// The deployment FileLock MUST already be locked before calling this function.
272	func (d *Deployment) rebuildFileOverviews() *broadcast.NginxAgentMessage {
273		fileOverviews := make([]*pb.File, 0, len(d.files))
274		for _, f := range d.files {
275			fileOverviews = append(fileOverviews, &pb.File{FileMeta: f.Meta})
276		}
277	
278		// Build the set of unmanaged files from volume mounts.
279		fileIgnoreSet := make(map[string]struct{})
280		for _, vm := range d.volumeMounts {
281			for _, f := range d.latestFileNames {
282				if strings.HasPrefix(f, vm.MountPath) {
283					fileIgnoreSet[f] = struct{}{}
284				}
285			}
286		}
287	
288		// Add static and volume-mount ignored files as 'unmanaged' so agent doesn't touch them.
289		for _, f := range ignoreFiles {
290			fileIgnoreSet[f] = struct{}{}
291		}
292	
293		for f := range fileIgnoreSet {
294			fileOverviews = append(fileOverviews, &pb.File{
295				FileMeta: &pb.FileMeta{
296					Name:        f,
297					Permissions: fileMode,
298				},
299				Unmanaged: true,
300			})
301		}
302	
303		newConfigVersion := filesHelper.GenerateConfigVersion(fileOverviews)
304		if d.configVersion == newConfigVersion {
305			// files have not changed, nothing to send
306			return nil
307		}
308	
309		d.configVersion = newConfigVersion
310		d.fileOverviews = fileOverviews
311	
312		return &broadcast.NginxAgentMessage{
313			Type:          broadcast.ConfigApplyRequest,
314			FileOverviews: fileOverviews,
315			ConfigVersion: d.configVersion,
316		}
317	}

321	// DeploymentStorer is an interface to store Deployments.
322	type DeploymentStorer interface {
323		Get(types.NamespacedName) *Deployment
324		GetOrStore(context.Context, types.NamespacedName, string) *Deployment
325		Remove(types.NamespacedName)
326	}
327	
328	// DeploymentStore holds a map of all Deployments.
329	type DeploymentStore struct {
330		connTracker agentgrpc.ConnectionsTracker
331		deployments sync.Map
332	}
333	
334	// NewDeploymentStore returns a new instance of a DeploymentStore.
335	func NewDeploymentStore(connTracker agentgrpc.ConnectionsTracker) *DeploymentStore {
336		return &DeploymentStore{
337			connTracker: connTracker,
338		}
339	}
340	
341	// Get returns the desired deployment from the store.
342	func (d *DeploymentStore) Get(nsName types.NamespacedName) *Deployment {
343		val, ok := d.deployments.Load(nsName)
344		if !ok {
345			return nil
346		}
347	
348		deployment, ok := val.(*Deployment)
349		if !ok {
350			panic(fmt.Sprintf("expected Deployment, got type %T", val))
351		}
352	
353		return deployment
354	}
355	
356	// GetOrStore returns the existing value for the key if present.
357	// Otherwise, it stores and returns the given value.
358	func (d *DeploymentStore) GetOrStore(
359		ctx context.Context,
360		nsName types.NamespacedName,
361		gatewayName string,
362	) *Deployment {
363		if deployment := d.Get(nsName); deployment != nil {
364			return deployment
365		}
366	
367		deployment := newDeployment(broadcast.NewDeploymentBroadcaster(ctx), gatewayName)
368		d.deployments.Store(nsName, deployment)
369	
370		return deployment
371	}
372	
373	// StoreWithBroadcaster creates a new Deployment with the supplied broadcaster and stores it.
374	// Used in unit tests to provide a mock broadcaster.
375	func (d *DeploymentStore) StoreWithBroadcaster(
376		nsName types.NamespacedName,
377		broadcaster broadcast.Broadcaster,
378		gatewayName string,
379	) *Deployment {
380		deployment := newDeployment(broadcaster, gatewayName)
381		d.deployments.Store(nsName, deployment)
382	
383		return deployment
384	}
385	
386	// Remove the deployment from the store.
387	func (d *DeploymentStore) Remove(nsName types.NamespacedName) {
388		d.deployments.Delete(nsName)
389	}

content.text: 现在我来阅读一下 `command.go`（即 `subscriber/agent` 连接处理器）、`messenger` 包、`filewatcher` 以及 `manager` 是如何将其整合在一起的。

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6		"fmt"
7		"io"
8		"strings"
9		"time"
10	
11		"github.com/go-logr/logr"
12		"github.com/google/uuid"
13		pb "github.com/nginx/agent/v3/api/grpc/mpi/v1"
14		"google.golang.org/grpc"
15		"google.golang.org/grpc/codes"
16		grpcStatus "google.golang.org/grpc/status"
17		"google.golang.org/protobuf/types/known/timestamppb"
18		appsv1 "k8s.io/api/apps/v1"
19		v1 "k8s.io/api/core/v1"
20		"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types"
21		"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/wait"
22		"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client"
23	
24		"github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/v2/internal/controller/nginx/agent/broadcast"
25		agentgrpc "github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/v2/internal/controller/nginx/agent/grpc"
26		grpcContext "github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/v2/internal/controller/nginx/agent/grpc/context"
27		"github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/v2/internal/controller/nginx/agent/grpc/messenger"
28		nginxTypes "github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/v2/internal/controller/nginx/types"
29		"github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/v2/internal/controller/status"
30	)
31	
32	const connectionWaitTimeout = 30 * time.Second
33	
34	// commandService handles the connection and subscription to the data plane agent.
35	type commandService struct {
36		pb.CommandServiceServer
37		nginxDeployments  *DeploymentStore
38		statusQueue       *status.Queue
39		resetConnChan     <-chan struct{}
40		connTracker       agentgrpc.ConnectionsTracker
41		k8sReader         client.Reader
42		logger            logr.Logger
43		connectionTimeout time.Duration
44	}
45	
46	func newCommandService(
47		logger logr.Logger,
48		reader client.Reader,
49		depStore *DeploymentStore,
50		connTracker agentgrpc.ConnectionsTracker,
51		statusQueue *status.Queue,
52		resetConnChan <-chan struct{},
53	) *commandService {
54		return &commandService{
55			connectionTimeout: connectionWaitTimeout,
56			k8sReader:         reader,
57			logger:            logger,
58			connTracker:       connTracker,
59			nginxDeployments:  depStore,
60			statusQueue:       statusQueue,
61			resetConnChan:     resetConnChan,
62		}
63	}
64	
65	func (cs *commandService) Register(server *grpc.Server) {
66		pb.RegisterCommandServiceServer(server, cs)
67	}
68	
69	// CreateConnection registers a data plane agent with the control plane.
70	// The nginx InstanceID could be empty if the agent hasn't discovered its nginx instance yet.
71	// Once discovered, the agent will send an UpdateDataPlaneStatus request with the nginx InstanceID set.
72	func (cs *commandService) CreateConnection(
73		ctx context.Context,
74		req *pb.CreateConnectionRequest,
75	) (*pb.CreateConnectionResponse, error) {
76		if req == nil {
77			return nil, errors.New("empty connection request")
78		}
79	
80		grpcInfo, ok := grpcContext.FromContext(ctx)
81		if !ok {
82			return nil, agentgrpc.ErrStatusInvalidConnection
83		}
84	
85		resource := req.GetResource()
86		podName := resource.GetContainerInfo().GetHostname()
87		cs.logger.Info(
88			fmt.Sprintf("Creating connection for nginx pod: %s", podName),
89			"correlation_id", req.GetMessageMeta().GetCorrelationId(),
90		)
91	
92		name, depType := getAgentDeploymentNameAndType(resource.GetInstances())
93		if name == (types.NamespacedName{}) || depType == "" {
94			err := errors.New("agent labels missing")
95			response := &pb.CreateConnectionResponse{
96				Response: &pb.CommandResponse{
97					Status:  pb.CommandResponse_COMMAND_STATUS_ERROR,
98					Message: "error getting pod owner",
99					Error:   err.Error(),
100				},
101			}
102			cs.logger.Error(err, "error getting pod owner", "correlation_id", req.GetMessageMeta().GetCorrelationId())
103			return response, grpcStatus.Errorf(codes.InvalidArgument, "error getting pod owner: %s", err.Error())
104		}
105	
106		conn := agentgrpc.Connection{
107			ParentName: name,
108			ParentType: depType,
109			InstanceID: getNginxInstanceID(resource.GetInstances()),
110		}
111		cs.connTracker.Track(grpcInfo.UUID, conn)
112	
113		return &pb.CreateConnectionResponse{
114			Response: &pb.CommandResponse{
115				Status: pb.CommandResponse_COMMAND_STATUS_OK,
116			},
117		}, nil
118	}
119	
120	// Subscribe is a decoupled communication mechanism between the data plane agent and control plane.
121	// The series of events are as follows:
122	// - Wait for the agent to register its nginx instance with the control plane.
123	// - Grab the most recent deployment configuration for itself, and attempt to apply it.
124	// - Subscribe to any future updates from the NginxUpdater and start a loop to listen for those updates.
125	// If any connection or unrecoverable errors occur, return and agent should re-establish a subscription.
126	// If errors occur with applying the config, log and put those errors into the status queue to be written
127	// to the Gateway status.
128	//
129	//nolint:gocyclo // could be room for improvement here
130	func (cs *commandService) Subscribe(in pb.CommandService_SubscribeServer) error {
131		ctx := in.Context()
132	
133		grpcInfo, ok := grpcContext.FromContext(ctx)
134		if !ok {
135			return agentgrpc.ErrStatusInvalidConnection
136		}
137		defer cs.connTracker.RemoveConnection(grpcInfo.UUID)
138	
139		// wait for the agent to report itself and nginx
140		conn, deployment, err := cs.waitForConnection(ctx, grpcInfo)
141		if err != nil {
142			cs.logger.Error(err, "error waiting for connection", "uuid", grpcInfo.UUID)
143			return err
144		}
145		defer deployment.RemovePodStatus(grpcInfo.UUID)
146	
147		cs.logger.Info(
148			"Successfully connected to nginx agent",
149			conn.ParentType, conn.ParentName,
150			"uuid", grpcInfo.UUID,
151		)
152	
153		msgr := messenger.New(in)
154		go msgr.Run(ctx)
155	
156		// Subscribe to broadcaster first, then apply current config before starting event loop
157		// Lock deployment for entire subscription + initial config process to prevent race conditions.
158		// Without this lock, the following race condition could occur:
159		// 1. New agent calls Subscribe() and starts setInitialConfig()
160		// 2. Concurrently, event handler calls broadcaster.Send() with a config update (file lock is held through
161		//    the entire broadcast transaction)
162		// 3. New agent gets stale config from setInitialConfig(), then subscribes
163		// 4. New agent misses the concurrent config update, leading to configuration drift
164		//
165		// By holding the lock across both broadcaster.Subscribe() and setInitialConfig(), we ensure atomicity:
166		// either the new subscriber gets the lock, subscribes and applies the latest config,
167		// then applies any concurrent updates that come in after the lock is released, or the subscriber gets the lock
168		// after the concurrent update, and applies that latest config in setInitialConfig() after subscribing,
169		// ensuring it doesn't miss any updates. Subscribing first also gives more time for the async subscription
170		// registration to complete before the lock is released.
171		deployment.FileLock.RLock()
172		// subscribe to the deployment broadcaster to get file updates
173		broadcaster := deployment.GetBroadcaster()
174		channels := broadcaster.Subscribe()
175	
176		if err := cs.setInitialConfig(ctx, &grpcInfo, deployment, conn, msgr); err != nil {
177			// Cancel subscription BEFORE releasing lock, this should help in cleaning
178			// up any channels or goroutines that are waiting on this subscription.
179			// This should also help in preventing the broadcaster from sending messages to this
180			// subscriber while we're trying to clean up and return due to the error.
181			broadcaster.CancelSubscription(channels.ID)
182			deployment.FileLock.RUnlock()
183			return err
184		}
185	
186		deployment.FileLock.RUnlock()
187		defer broadcaster.CancelSubscription(channels.ID)
188	
189		var pendingBroadcastRequest *broadcast.NginxAgentMessage
190	
191		for {
192			// When a message is received over the ListenCh, it is assumed and required that the
193			// deployment object is already LOCKED. This lock is acquired by the event handler before calling
194			// `updateNginxConfig`. The entire transaction (as described in above in the function comment)
195			// must be locked to prevent the deployment files from changing during the transaction.
196			// This means that the lock is held until we receive either an error or response from agent
197			// (via msgr.Errors() or msgr.Messages()) and respond back, finally returning to the event handler
198			// which releases the lock.
199			select {
200			case <-ctx.Done():
201				select {
202				case channels.ResponseCh <- struct{}{}:
203				default:
204				}
205				return grpcStatus.Error(codes.Canceled, context.Cause(ctx).Error())
206			case <-cs.resetConnChan:
207				return grpcStatus.Error(codes.Unavailable, "TLS files updated")
208			case msg := <-channels.ListenCh:
209				var req *pb.ManagementPlaneRequest
210				switch msg.Type {
211				case broadcast.ConfigApplyRequest:
212					req = buildRequest(msg.FileOverviews, conn.InstanceID, msg.ConfigVersion)
213					cs.logger.V(1).Info(
214						"Sending configuration to agent",
215						"requestType", msg.Type,
216						"uuid", grpcInfo.UUID,
217						"correlation_id", req.GetMessageMeta().GetCorrelationId(),
218						"number_of_files", len(msg.FileOverviews),
219					)
220				case broadcast.APIRequest:
221					req = buildPlusAPIRequest(msg.NGINXPlusAction, conn.InstanceID)
222					cs.logger.V(1).Info(
223						"Sending configuration to agent",
224						"requestType", msg.Type,
225						"uuid", grpcInfo.UUID,
226						"correlation_id", req.GetMessageMeta().GetCorrelationId(),
227					)
228				default:
229					panic(fmt.Sprintf("unknown request type %d", msg.Type))
230				}
231	
232				if err := msgr.Send(ctx, req); err != nil {
233					cs.logger.Error(err, "error sending request to agent")
234					deployment.SetPodErrorStatus(grpcInfo.UUID, err)
235					channels.ResponseCh <- struct{}{}
236	
237					return grpcStatus.Error(codes.Internal, err.Error())
238				}
239	
240				// Track this broadcast request to distinguish it from initial config operations.
241				// Only broadcast operations should signal ResponseCh for coordination.
242				pendingBroadcastRequest = &msg
243			case err = <-msgr.Errors():
244				cs.logger.Error(err, "connection error", conn.ParentType, conn.ParentName, "uuid", grpcInfo.UUID)
245				deployment.SetPodErrorStatus(grpcInfo.UUID, err)
246				select {
247				case channels.ResponseCh <- struct{}{}:
248				default:
249				}
250				if pendingBroadcastRequest != nil {
251					cs.logger.V(1).Info("Connection error during pending request, operation failed", "uuid", grpcInfo.UUID)
252				}
253	
254				if errors.Is(err, io.EOF) {
255					return grpcStatus.Error(codes.Aborted, err.Error())
256				}
257				return grpcStatus.Error(codes.Internal, err.Error())
258			case msg := <-msgr.Messages():
259				res := msg.GetCommandResponse()
260				if res.GetStatus() != pb.CommandResponse_COMMAND_STATUS_OK {
261					if isRollbackMessage(res.GetMessage()) {
262						// we don't care about these messages, so ignore them
263						continue
264					}
265					err := fmt.Errorf("msg: %s; error: %s", res.GetMessage(), res.GetError())
266					deployment.SetPodErrorStatus(grpcInfo.UUID, err)
267				} else {
268					deployment.SetPodErrorStatus(grpcInfo.UUID, nil)
269				}
270	
271				// Signal broadcast completion only for tracked broadcast operations.
272				// Initial config responses are ignored to prevent spurious success messages.
273				if pendingBroadcastRequest != nil {
274					pendingBroadcastRequest = nil
275					channels.ResponseCh <- struct{}{}
276				} else {
277					cs.logger.V(1).Info(
278						"Received response for non-broadcast request (likely initial config)",
279						conn.ParentType, conn.ParentName,
280						"uuid", grpcInfo.UUID,
281					)
282				}
283			}
284		}
285	}
286	
287	func (cs *commandService) waitForConnection(
288		ctx context.Context,
289		grpcInfo grpcContext.GrpcInfo,
290	) (*agentgrpc.Connection, *Deployment, error) {
291		ticker := time.NewTicker(time.Second)
292		defer ticker.Stop()
293	
294		timer := time.NewTimer(cs.connectionTimeout)
295		defer timer.Stop()
296	
297		agentConnectErr := errors.New("timed out waiting for agent to register nginx")
298		deploymentStoreErr := errors.New("timed out waiting for nginx deployment to be added to store")
299	
300		var err error
301		for {
302			select {
303			case <-ctx.Done():
304				return nil, nil, ctx.Err()
305			case <-timer.C:
306				return nil, nil, err
307			case <-ticker.C:
308				if conn := cs.connTracker.GetConnection(grpcInfo.UUID); conn.Ready() {
309					// connection has been established, now ensure that the deployment exists in the store
310					if deployment := cs.nginxDeployments.Get(conn.ParentName); deployment != nil {
311						return &conn, deployment, nil
312					}
313					err = deploymentStoreErr
314					continue
315				}
316				err = agentConnectErr
317			}
318		}
319	}
320	
321	// setInitialConfig gets the initial configuration for this connection and applies it.
322	// The deployment FileLock MUST already be locked before calling this function.
323	func (cs *commandService) setInitialConfig(
324		ctx context.Context,
325		grpcInfo *grpcContext.GrpcInfo,
326		deployment *Deployment,
327		conn *agentgrpc.Connection,
328		msgr messenger.Messenger,
329	) error {
330		if err := cs.validatePodImageVersion(conn.ParentName, conn.ParentType, deployment.imageVersion); err != nil {
331			cs.logAndSendErrorStatus(grpcInfo, deployment, conn, err)
332			return grpcStatus.Errorf(codes.FailedPrecondition, "nginx image version validation failed: %s", err.Error())
333		}
334	
335		fileOverviews, configVersion := deployment.GetFileOverviews()
336	
337		cs.logger.Info(
338			"Sending initial configuration to agent",
339			conn.ParentType, conn.ParentName,
340			"uuid", grpcInfo.UUID,
341			"configVersion", configVersion,
342		)
343	
344		if err := msgr.Send(ctx, buildRequest(fileOverviews, conn.InstanceID, configVersion)); err != nil {
345			cs.logAndSendErrorStatus(grpcInfo, deployment, conn, err)
346	
347			return grpcStatus.Error(codes.Internal, err.Error())
348		}
349	
350		applyErr, connErr := cs.waitForInitialConfigApply(ctx, msgr)
351		if connErr != nil {
352			cs.logger.Error(connErr, "error setting initial configuration", "uuid", grpcInfo.UUID)
353	
354			return connErr
355		}
356	
357		errs := []error{applyErr}
358		for _, action := range deployment.GetNGINXPlusActions() {
359			// retry the API update request because sometimes nginx isn't quite ready after the config apply reload
360			timeoutCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 30*time.Second)
361			var overallUpstreamApplyErr error
362	
363			if err := wait.PollUntilContextCancel(
364				timeoutCtx,
365				500*time.Millisecond,
366				true, // poll immediately
367				func(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {
368					if err := msgr.Send(ctx, buildPlusAPIRequest(action, conn.InstanceID)); err != nil {
369						cs.logAndSendErrorStatus(grpcInfo, deployment, conn, err)
370	
371						return false, grpcStatus.Error(codes.Internal, err.Error())
372					}
373	
374					upstreamApplyErr, connErr := cs.waitForInitialConfigApply(ctx, msgr)
375					if connErr != nil {
376						cs.logger.Error(connErr, "error setting initial configuration", "uuid", grpcInfo.UUID)
377	
378						return false, connErr
379					}
380	
381					if upstreamApplyErr != nil {
382						overallUpstreamApplyErr = errors.Join(overallUpstreamApplyErr, upstreamApplyErr)
383						return false, nil
384					}
385					return true, nil
386				},
387			); err != nil {
388				if overallUpstreamApplyErr != nil {
389					errs = append(errs, overallUpstreamApplyErr)
390				} else {
391					cancel()
392					return err
393				}
394			}
395			cancel()
396		}
397		// send the status (error or nil) to the status queue
398		cs.logAndSendErrorStatus(grpcInfo, deployment, conn, errors.Join(errs...))
399	
400		return nil
401	}
402	
403	// waitForInitialConfigApply waits for the nginx agent to respond after a Subscriber attempts
404	// to apply its initial config.
405	// Two errors are returned
406	// - applyErr is an error applying the configuration
407	// - connectionErr is an error with the connection or sending the configuration
408	// The caller treats a connectionErr as unrecoverable, while the applyErr is used
409	// to set the status on the Gateway resources.
410	func (cs *commandService) waitForInitialConfigApply(
411		ctx context.Context,
412		msgr messenger.Messenger,
413	) (applyErr error, connectionErr error) {
414		for {
415			select {
416			case <-ctx.Done():
417				return nil, grpcStatus.Error(codes.Canceled, context.Cause(ctx).Error())
418			case err := <-msgr.Errors():
419				if errors.Is(err, io.EOF) {
420					return nil, grpcStatus.Error(codes.Aborted, err.Error())
421				}
422				return nil, grpcStatus.Error(codes.Internal, err.Error())
423			case msg := <-msgr.Messages():
424				res := msg.GetCommandResponse()
425				if res.GetStatus() != pb.CommandResponse_COMMAND_STATUS_OK {
426					applyErr := fmt.Errorf("msg: %s; error: %s", res.GetMessage(), res.GetError())
427					cs.logger.V(1).Info("Received initial config response with error", "error", applyErr)
428					return applyErr, nil
429				}
430	
431				cs.logger.V(1).Info("Received successful initial config response")
432				return applyErr, connectionErr
433			}
434		}
435	}
436	
437	// logAndSendErrorStatus logs an error, sets it on the Deployment object for that Pod, and then sends
438	// the full Deployment error status to the status queue. This ensures that any other Pod errors that already
439	// exist on the Deployment are not overwritten.
440	// If the error is nil, then we just enqueue the nil value and don't log it, which indicates success.
441	func (cs *commandService) logAndSendErrorStatus(
442		grpcInfo *grpcContext.GrpcInfo,
443		deployment *Deployment,
444		conn *agentgrpc.Connection,
445		err error,
446	) {
447		if err != nil {
448			cs.logger.Error(err, "error sending request to agent", "uuid", grpcInfo.UUID)
449		} else {
450			cs.logger.Info(
451				"Successfully configured nginx for new subscription",
452				conn.ParentType, conn.ParentName,
453				"uuid", grpcInfo.UUID,
454			)
455		}
456		deployment.SetPodErrorStatus(grpcInfo.UUID, err)
457	
458		queueObj := &status.QueueObject{
459			Deployment: status.Deployment{
460				NamespacedName: conn.ParentName,
461				GatewayName:    deployment.gatewayName,
462			},
463			Error:             deployment.GetConfigurationStatus(),
464			UpdateType:        status.UpdateAll,
465			NginxConfigPushed: true,
466		}
467		cs.statusQueue.Enqueue(queueObj)
468	}
469	
470	func buildRequest(fileOverviews []*pb.File, instanceID, version string) *pb.ManagementPlaneRequest {
471		return &pb.ManagementPlaneRequest{
472			MessageMeta: &pb.MessageMeta{
473				MessageId:     uuid.NewString(),
474				CorrelationId: uuid.NewString(),
475				Timestamp:     timestamppb.Now(),
476			},
477			Request: &pb.ManagementPlaneRequest_ConfigApplyRequest{
478				ConfigApplyRequest: &pb.ConfigApplyRequest{
479					Overview: &pb.FileOverview{
480						Files: fileOverviews,
481						ConfigVersion: &pb.ConfigVersion{
482							InstanceId: instanceID,
483							Version:    version,
484						},
485					},
486				},
487			},
488		}
489	}
490	
491	func isRollbackMessage(msg string) bool {
492		msgToLower := strings.ToLower(msg)
493		return strings.Contains(msgToLower, "rollback successful") ||
494			strings.Contains(msgToLower, "rollback failed")
495	}
496	
497	func buildPlusAPIRequest(action *pb.NGINXPlusAction, instanceID string) *pb.ManagementPlaneRequest {
498		return &pb.ManagementPlaneRequest{
499			MessageMeta: &pb.MessageMeta{
500				MessageId:     uuid.NewString(),
501				CorrelationId: uuid.NewString(),
502				Timestamp:     timestamppb.Now(),
503			},
504			Request: &pb.ManagementPlaneRequest_ActionRequest{
505				ActionRequest: &pb.APIActionRequest{
506					InstanceId: instanceID,
507					Action: &pb.APIActionRequest_NginxPlusAction{
508						NginxPlusAction: action,
509					},
510				},
511			},
512		}
513	}
514	
515	// validatePodImageVersion checks if the pod's nginx container image version matches the expected version
516	// from its deployment. Returns an error if versions don't match.
517	func (cs *commandService) validatePodImageVersion(
518		parent types.NamespacedName,
519		parentType string,
520		expectedImage string,
521	) error {
522		var nginxImage string
523		var found bool
524	
525		getNginxContainerImage := func(containers []v1.Container) (string, bool) {
526			for _, c := range containers {
527				if c.Name == "nginx" {
528					return c.Image, true
529				}
530			}
531			return "", false
532		}
533	
534		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second)
535		defer cancel()
536	
537		switch parentType {
538		case nginxTypes.DaemonSetType:
539			ds := &appsv1.DaemonSet{}
540			if err := cs.k8sReader.Get(ctx, parent, ds); err != nil {
541				return fmt.Errorf("failed to get DaemonSet %s: %w", parent.String(), err)
542			}
543			nginxImage, found = getNginxContainerImage(ds.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers)
544		case nginxTypes.DeploymentType:
545			deploy := &appsv1.Deployment{}
546			if err := cs.k8sReader.Get(ctx, parent, deploy); err != nil {
547				return fmt.Errorf("failed to get Deployment %s: %w", parent.String(), err)
548			}
549			nginxImage, found = getNginxContainerImage(deploy.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers)
550		default:
551			return fmt.Errorf("unknown parentType: %s", parentType)
552		}
553	
554		if !found {
555			return fmt.Errorf("nginx container not found in %s %q", parentType, parent.Name)
556		}
557	
558		if nginxImage != expectedImage {
559			return fmt.Errorf("nginx image version mismatch: has %q but expected %q", nginxImage, expectedImage)
560		}
561	
562		cs.logger.V(1).Info("nginx image version validated successfully",
563			"parent", parent.String(),
564			"image", nginxImage)
565	
566		return nil
567	}
568	
569	// UpdateDataPlaneStatus is called by agent on startup and upon any change in agent metadata,
570	// instance metadata, or configurations. InstanceID may not be set on an initial CreateConnection,
571	// and will instead be set on a call to UpdateDataPlaneStatus once the agent discovers its nginx instance.
572	func (cs *commandService) UpdateDataPlaneStatus(
573		ctx context.Context,
574		req *pb.UpdateDataPlaneStatusRequest,
575	) (*pb.UpdateDataPlaneStatusResponse, error) {
576		if req == nil {
577			return nil, errors.New("empty UpdateDataPlaneStatus request")
578		}
579	
580		grpcInfo, ok := grpcContext.FromContext(ctx)
581		if !ok {
582			return nil, agentgrpc.ErrStatusInvalidConnection
583		}
584	
585		instanceID := getNginxInstanceID(req.GetResource().GetInstances())
586		if instanceID == "" {
587			return nil, grpcStatus.Errorf(codes.InvalidArgument, "request does not contain nginx instanceID")
588		}
589	
590		cs.connTracker.SetInstanceID(grpcInfo.UUID, instanceID)
591	
592		return &pb.UpdateDataPlaneStatusResponse{}, nil
593	}
594	
595	func getNginxInstanceID(instances []*pb.Instance) string {
596		for _, instance := range instances {
597			instanceType := instance.GetInstanceMeta().GetInstanceType()
598			if instanceType == pb.InstanceMeta_INSTANCE_TYPE_NGINX ||
599				instanceType == pb.InstanceMeta_INSTANCE_TYPE_NGINX_PLUS {
600				return instance.GetInstanceMeta().GetInstanceId()
601			}
602		}
603	
604		return ""
605	}
606	
607	func getAgentDeploymentNameAndType(instances []*pb.Instance) (types.NamespacedName, string) {
608		var nsName types.NamespacedName
609		var depType string
610	
611		for _, instance := range instances {
612			instanceType := instance.GetInstanceMeta().GetInstanceType()
613			if instanceType == pb.InstanceMeta_INSTANCE_TYPE_AGENT {
614				labels := instance.GetInstanceConfig().GetAgentConfig().GetLabels()
615	
616				for _, label := range labels {
617					fields := label.GetFields()
618	
619					if val, ok := fields[nginxTypes.AgentOwnerNameLabel]; ok {
620						fullName := val.GetStringValue()
621						parts := strings.SplitN(fullName, "_", 2)
622						if len(parts) == 2 {
623							nsName = types.NamespacedName{Namespace: parts[0], Name: parts[1]}
624						}
625					}
626					if val, ok := fields[nginxTypes.AgentOwnerTypeLabel]; ok {
627						depType = val.GetStringValue()
628					}
629				}
630			}
631		}
632	
633		return nsName, depType
634	}
635	
636	// UpdateDataPlaneHealth includes full health information about the data plane as reported by the agent.
637	func (*commandService) UpdateDataPlaneHealth(
638		context.Context,
639		*pb.UpdateDataPlaneHealthRequest,
640	) (*pb.UpdateDataPlaneHealthResponse, error) {
641		return &pb.UpdateDataPlaneHealthResponse{}, nil
642	}

1	package interceptor
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"strings"
7		"time"
8	
9		"github.com/go-logr/logr"
10		"google.golang.org/grpc"
11		"google.golang.org/grpc/codes"
12		"google.golang.org/grpc/metadata"
13		"google.golang.org/grpc/status"
14		authv1 "k8s.io/api/authentication/v1"
15		corev1 "k8s.io/api/core/v1"
16		"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels"
17		"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/wait"
18		"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client"
19	
20		grpcContext "github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/v2/internal/controller/nginx/agent/grpc/context"
21		"github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/v2/internal/framework/controller"
22	)
23	
24	const (
25		headerUUID = "uuid"
26		headerAuth = "authorization"
27	)
28	
29	// PodCheckRetry controls how long validateToken will wait for the agent's Pod
30	// to appear as Running in the cache. This handles a startup race where the
31	// agent dials before NGF's Pod informer reflects the new Pod (for example, when
32	// a WAF-enabled NGINX Pod starts and the agent dials before NGF has seen the
33	// Pod's Running status, or while sidecars like waf-config-mgr are still
34	// initializing).
35	type PodCheckRetry struct {
36		// PollInterval is the interval between Pod list attempts.
37		PollInterval time.Duration
38		// Timeout is the total time spent polling before giving up.
39		Timeout time.Duration
40	}
41	
42	var defaultPodCheckRetry = PodCheckRetry{
43		PollInterval: 500 * time.Millisecond,
44		Timeout:      15 * time.Second,
45	}
46	
47	// streamHandler is a struct that implements StreamHandler, allowing the interceptor to replace the context.
48	type streamHandler struct {
49		grpc.ServerStream
50		ctx context.Context
51	}
52	
53	func (sh *streamHandler) Context() context.Context {
54		return sh.ctx
55	}
56	
57	type ContextSetter struct {
58		k8sClient client.Client
59		audience  string
60		podCheck  PodCheckRetry
61	}
62	
63	func NewContextSetter(k8sClient client.Client, audience string) ContextSetter {
64		return ContextSetter{
65			k8sClient: k8sClient,
66			audience:  audience,
67			podCheck:  defaultPodCheckRetry,
68		}
69	}
70	
71	func (c ContextSetter) Stream(logger logr.Logger) grpc.StreamServerInterceptor {
72		return func(
73			srv any,
74			ss grpc.ServerStream,
75			_ *grpc.StreamServerInfo,
76			handler grpc.StreamHandler,
77		) error {
78			ctx, err := c.validateConnection(ss.Context(), logger)
79			if err != nil {
80				logger.Error(err, "error validating connection")
81				return err
82			}
83			return handler(srv, &streamHandler{
84				ServerStream: ss,
85				ctx:          ctx,
86			})
87		}
88	}
89	
90	func (c ContextSetter) Unary(logger logr.Logger) grpc.UnaryServerInterceptor {
91		return func(
92			ctx context.Context,
93			req any,
94			_ *grpc.UnaryServerInfo,
95			handler grpc.UnaryHandler,
96		) (resp any, err error) {
97			if ctx, err = c.validateConnection(ctx, logger); err != nil {
98				logger.Error(err, "error validating connection")
99				return nil, err
100			}
101			return handler(ctx, req)
102		}
103	}
104	
105	// validateConnection checks that the connection is valid and returns a new
106	// context containing information used by the gRPC command/file services.
107	func (c ContextSetter) validateConnection(ctx context.Context, logger logr.Logger) (context.Context, error) {
108		grpcInfo, err := getGrpcInfo(ctx)
109		if err != nil {
110			return nil, err
111		}
112	
113		return c.validateToken(ctx, grpcInfo, logger)
114	}
115	
116	func getGrpcInfo(ctx context.Context) (*grpcContext.GrpcInfo, error) {
117		md, ok := metadata.FromIncomingContext(ctx)
118		if !ok {
119			return nil, status.Error(codes.InvalidArgument, "no metadata")
120		}
121	
122		id := md.Get(headerUUID)
123		if len(id) == 0 {
124			return nil, status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, "no identity")
125		}
126	
127		auths := md.Get(headerAuth)
128		if len(auths) == 0 {
129			return nil, status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, "no authorization")
130		}
131	
132		return &grpcContext.GrpcInfo{
133			UUID:  id[0],
134			Token: auths[0],
135		}, nil
136	}
137	
138	func (c ContextSetter) validateToken(
139		ctx context.Context,
140		grpcInfo *grpcContext.GrpcInfo,
141		logger logr.Logger,
142	) (context.Context, error) {
143		tokenReview := &authv1.TokenReview{
144			Spec: authv1.TokenReviewSpec{
145				Audiences: []string{c.audience},
146				Token:     grpcInfo.Token,
147			},
148		}
149	
150		createCtx, createCancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 30*time.Second)
151		defer createCancel()
152	
153		if err := c.k8sClient.Create(createCtx, tokenReview); err != nil {
154			return nil, status.Error(codes.Internal, fmt.Sprintf("error creating TokenReview: %v", err))
155		}
156	
157		if !tokenReview.Status.Authenticated {
158			return nil, status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, fmt.Sprintf("invalid authorization: %s", tokenReview.Status.Error))
159		}
160	
161		usernameItems := strings.Split(tokenReview.Status.User.Username, ":")
162		if len(usernameItems) != 4 || usernameItems[0] != "system" || usernameItems[1] != "serviceaccount" {
163			msg := fmt.Sprintf(
164				"token username must be of the format 'system:serviceaccount:NAMESPACE:NAME': %s",
165				tokenReview.Status.User.Username,
166			)
167			return nil, status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, msg)
168		}
169	
170		saNamespace := usernameItems[2]
171		saName := usernameItems[3]
172	
173		opts := &client.ListOptions{
174			Namespace: saNamespace,
175			LabelSelector: labels.Set(map[string]string{
176				controller.AppNameLabel: saName,
177			}).AsSelector(),
178		}
179	
180		if err := c.waitForRunningPod(ctx, logger, opts, saNamespace, saName); err != nil {
181			return nil, err
182		}
183	
184		return grpcContext.NewGrpcContext(ctx, *grpcInfo), nil
185	}
186	
187	// waitForRunningPod polls for a Running Pod that matches the agent's
188	// ServiceAccount. This absorbs a brief startup race where the agent dials
189	// before NGF's cache has observed the new Pod as Running (e.g. when slower
190	// sidecars like waf-config-mgr delay full Pod readiness). Authentication
191	// failures are returned immediately by validateToken; this helper only
192	// handles the "no running pods yet" transient case.
193	func (c ContextSetter) waitForRunningPod(
194		ctx context.Context,
195		logger logr.Logger,
196		opts *client.ListOptions,
197		saNamespace, saName string,
198	) error {
199		retry := c.podCheck
200		if retry.PollInterval <= 0 || retry.Timeout <= 0 {
201			retry = defaultPodCheckRetry
202		}
203	
204		waitCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, retry.Timeout)
205		defer cancel()
206	
207		start := time.Now()
208		var (
209			attempt int
210			listErr error
211		)
212	
213		pollErr := wait.PollUntilContextCancel(waitCtx, retry.PollInterval, true,
214			func(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {
215				attempt++
216				var podList corev1.PodList
217				if err := c.k8sClient.List(ctx, &podList, opts); err != nil {
218					listErr = status.Error(codes.Internal, fmt.Sprintf("error listing pods: %s", err.Error()))
219					return false, listErr
220				}
221	
222				for _, pod := range podList.Items {
223					if pod.Status.Phase == corev1.PodRunning {
224						return true, nil
225					}
226				}
227	
228				logger.V(1).Info(
229					"no running pods found for agent service account; retrying",
230					"namespace", saNamespace,
231					"serviceAccount", saName,
232					"attempt", attempt,
233					"elapsed", time.Since(start).String(),
234				)
235				return false, nil
236			},
237		)
238		if pollErr == nil {
239			return nil
240		}
241		// If the wait was interrupted (deadline/cancel) rather than the condition
242		// returning a hard error, surface the startup-not-ready signal as
243		// Unavailable so callers can retry; otherwise propagate the List error.
244		if wait.Interrupted(pollErr) {
245			return status.Error(codes.Unavailable, fmt.Sprintf(
246				"no running pods found for service account %s/%s after %d attempts (%s); "+
247					"the agent's Pod may still be starting",
248				saNamespace, saName, attempt, time.Since(start),
249			))
250		}
251		if listErr != nil {
252			return listErr
253		}
254		return pollErr
255	}
256

input.content:
# NginxUpdater 与 gRPC Agent 通信

> 前置：本讲承接 [u6-l1 配置生成器总览](u6-l1-config-generator-overview.md)。配置生成器 `generator.Generate(conf)` 已经把 `dataplane.Configuration` 渲染成了一组 `[]agent.File`。本讲要回答的核心问题是：**这些配置文件是怎么从控制面（Go 进程）一路送到数据面（NGINX Pod）里的？** 答案是一条以 NGINX Agent 为客户端、控制面为服务端的 gRPC 通道，以及一套自研的「广播 + 订阅」消息机制。

## 1. 本讲目标

学完本讲，你应当能够：

- 说清 `NginxUpdater` 接口的职责，以及 `UpdateConfig` 在一次配置更新里做了哪三件事。
- 描述控制面 gRPC 服务是如何启动的、注册了哪些 gRPC service、用什么样的安全（mTLS + TokenReview）保护连接。
- 解释 `ConnectionsTracker` 如何用 UUID 追踪每一条 Agent 连接，以及连接「就绪」的判定标准。
- 复述「广播者（Broadcaster）→ 订阅者（Subscribe 双向流）→ Messenger → Agent」这条完整消息路径，以及 Agent 反向拉文件（`GetFile`）的二次往返。
- 跟踪一次配置更新从 `UpdateConfig` 到 Agent 收到请求的完整调用链。

## 2. 前置知识

阅读本讲前，建议先理解下面几个概念：

- **控制面 / 数据面分离**：NGF 的 Go 进程只负责 watch K8s 资源、生成配置；真正处理流量的 NGINX 跑在另一个 Pod 里。两者必须有一条通信通道把配置送过去。详见 [u1-l1 项目概览](u1-l1-project-overview.md)。
- **NGINX Agent**：这是 F5 提供的一个独立组件（不是本仓库的代码），以 sidecar 形式和数据面 NGINX 跑在同一个 Pod 里。它作为一个 gRPC **客户端**，主动 **拨号** 连接到控制面的 gRPC 服务端，接收配置并触发 NGINX reload。注意方向：是数据面连控制面，不是控制面连数据面。
- **gRPC 双向流（bidirectional streaming）**：客户端和服务端各自持有一条长连接的流，双方都可以随时在上面发消息。NGF 用 `CommandService.Subscribe` 这一条双向流作为「控制面 → Agent」的主信道。
- **MPI（Management Plane Interface）协议**：NGINX Agent v3 用的 gRPC 协议，定义在 `github.com/nginx/agent/v3/api/grpc/mpi/v1`（简称 `pb`）。本讲会遇到 `CommandService`、`FileService`、`ManagementPlaneRequest`、`DataPlaneResponse`、`ConfigApplyRequest` 等类型，都来自这个包。
- **mTLS**：双向 TLS。控制面和 Agent 互相验证对方证书，共享同一个 CA。证书由 `generate-certs` 命令生成，详见 [u2-l3 初始化命令与证书生成](u2-l3-initialize-and-generate-certs.md)。
- **manager.Runnable**：controller-runtime 的抽象，表示「一个要被 Manager 管理生命周期的长跑 goroutine」。gRPC 服务就是以 Runnable 形式注册进 Manager 的。详见 [u3-l4 Leader Election 与 Runnables](u3-l4-leader-election-and-runnables.md)。

一个关键直觉先建立起来：NGF 的控制面 **不直接** 把配置写进 NGINX Pod 的磁盘，而是把「文件清单（file overviews，含名字/大小/hash）」通过一条长连接推给 Agent；Agent 拿到清单后，发现自己缺哪些文件，再 **反向** 用 `GetFile` / `GetFileStream` 这两个 RPC 把文件内容按需拉回去。这是一种「推元数据 + 拉内容」的混合模式，好处是大文件可以分块流式拉取，且 Agent 可以做增量比对。

## 3. 本讲源码地图

本讲涉及的关键文件：

| 文件 | 作用 |
| --- | --- |
| `internal/controller/nginx/agent/agent.go` | 定义 `NginxUpdater` 接口与其实现 `NginxUpdaterImpl`，是配置下发的**总入口**。 |
| `internal/controller/nginx/agent/deployment.go` | 定义 `Deployment`（一个数据面 Deployment 的运行时状态：文件、版本、错误）与 `DeploymentStore`。`SetFiles` 计算配置版本、产出广播消息。 |
| `internal/controller/nginx/agent/broadcast/broadcast.go` | 自研的「广播者」：把一条配置消息扇出（fan-out）给该 Deployment 下所有已订阅的 Agent。 |
| `internal/controller/nginx/agent/command.go` | `commandService`，实现 gRPC `CommandService`。`Subscribe` 是每个 Agent 连接的核心循环。 |
| `internal/controller/nginx/agent/grpc/messenger/messenger.go` | `Messenger`，把一条 gRPC 双向流封装成「发 / 收」两个 channel，解耦读写。 |
| `internal/controller/nginx/agent/file.go` | `fileService`，实现 gRPC `FileService`。`GetFile` / `GetFileStream` 供 Agent 反向拉文件内容。 |
| `internal/controller/nginx/agent/grpc/grpc.go` | gRPC **服务端** 的创建、mTLS 配置、服务注册、TLS 文件热加载。 |
| `internal/controller/nginx/agent/grpc/connections.go` | `ConnectionsTracker`，按 UUID 追踪所有 Agent 连接。 |
| `internal/controller/nginx/agent/grpc/filewatcher/filewatcher.go` | 监听 TLS 证书文件变化，证书轮换时触发所有 Agent 重连。 |
| `internal/controller/nginx/agent/grpc/interceptor/interceptor.go` | gRPC 拦截器：做 TokenReview 身份校验，把连接身份注入 context。 |
| `internal/controller/manager.go` | `createAgentServices`：把上述组件装配起来并注册进 Manager。 |

一个分层心智模型（从上到下是调用方向）：

```
EventHandler (handler.go)
        │  UpdateConfig(deployment, files, vm)
        ▼
NginxUpdaterImpl (agent.go)          ← 第 1 层：接口入口
        │  deployment.SetFiles(...)  → 计算版本，产出消息
        │  broadcaster.Send(msg)     → 扇出给所有订阅者
        ▼
DeploymentBroadcaster (broadcast.go) ← 第 3 层：消息广播
        │  往每个 listener 的 listenCh 投递消息
        ▼
commandService.Subscribe (command.go)← 第 3 层：每个 Agent 一个循环
        │  收到 msg → buildRequest → msgr.Send
        ▼
Messenger (messenger.go)             ← 读写解耦
        │  server.Send(ManagementPlaneRequest)  走 gRPC 流
        ▼
NGINX Agent (数据面，外部组件)
        │  收到 ConfigApplyRequest，发现缺文件
        │  反向调用 GetFile / GetFileStream
        ▼
fileService.GetFile (file.go)        ← 反向信道：按需拉文件
        │  返回文件内容
        ▼
NGINX Agent 写盘 + reload，回 DataPlaneResponse
```

## 4. 核心概念与源码讲解

本讲拆成三个最小模块：**NginxUpdater 接口**（4.1）、**gRPC 服务与连接**（4.2）、**消息广播**（4.3）。三者串起来就是一次完整的下发链路。

### 4.1 NginxUpdater 接口：配置下发的总入口

#### 4.1.1 概念说明

`NginxUpdater` 是控制面对「把配置送给数据面」这件事的抽象。它对上游（eventHandler）只暴露两个方法：

- `UpdateConfig`：下发**完整的一组配置文件**（OSS 和 Plus 都用，会触发 NGINX reload）。
- `UpdateUpstreamServers`：**仅 Plus**，用 NGINX Plus 的动态 API 增量改 upstream server 列表（不 reload）。

为什么要抽象成接口？因为 eventHandler 需要在测试里用 fake 替换它（注意源码里 `//counterfeiter:generate . NginxUpdater` 这行注释，counterfeiter 会据此生成 mock），从而单测不需要真起 gRPC 服务。

一个关键设计：`NginxUpdater` 自己**不知道**有哪些 Agent 连上来了，也**不直接**发 gRPC。它只做两件事——算出「这次配置和上次比有没有变」，变了就把消息丢给一个 `Broadcaster`。真正和 Agent 通信是 Broadcaster + commandService 的事（见 4.3）。这种分层让「配置生成」和「网络通信」彻底解耦。

#### 4.1.2 核心流程

`UpdateConfig` 的流程，源码注释里写得很清楚：

1. **设置文件并计算版本**：`deployment.SetFiles(files, volumeMounts)` 把新文件存进 `Deployment`，并重新计算一个 `configVersion`（文件清单的 hash）。如果版本没变，返回 `nil`——意味着「配置其实没变」，直接 return，**什么都不发**。这是把 N 次无意义变更压成 0 次下发的关键。
2. **广播消息**：版本变了，就 `deployment.GetBroadcaster().Send(*msg)`，把包含文件元数据（名字、大小、hash，**不含内容**）的消息扇出给所有订阅的 Agent。
3. **登记错误**：`deployment.SetLatestConfigError(deployment.GetConfigurationStatus())`，把「各 Pod 上一次 apply 的聚合错误」记下来，供后续写 Gateway status 用。

`UpdateUpstreamServers`（仅 Plus）流程类似但更复杂：它把每个 upstream 翻译成一条 `NGINXPlusAction`（动态增删 server），逐条广播，并带 5 秒重试（因为 reload 后 NGINX 可能还没就绪）。

一个把「发消息」和「等结果」分离的设计点：`broadcaster.Send` 是**同步**的——它会阻塞到所有订阅者都回复（或出错）才返回。这样 `UpdateConfig` 返回时，配置已经下发完毕（成功或失败），eventHandler 才能据此写正确的 status。

#### 4.1.3 源码精读

先看接口定义和实现结构：

[NginxUpdater 接口定义（agent.go:31-35）](https://github.com/nginxinc/nginx-gateway-fabric/blob/5504d0d7de1706d1bda281281fbb0753a7b32806/internal/controller/nginx/agent/agent.go#L31-L35) —— 两个方法，一个是整组文件下发，一个是 Plus 专属的 upstream 动态更新。

[NewNginxUpdater 工厂（agent.go:47-76）](https://github.com/nginxinc/nginx-gateway-fabric/blob/5504d0d7de1706d1bda281281fbb0753a7b32806/internal/controller/nginx/agent/agent.go#L47-L76) —— 这里能看出它内部由三块拼成：`ConnectionsTracker`（连接表）、`DeploymentStore`（按 Deployment 组织文件）、`commandService` + `fileService`（两个 gRPC service）。`resetConnChan` 是证书轮换时通知重连的通道。注意 `commandService` 和 `fileService` 共享同一个 `nginxDeployments` 和 `connTracker`——这是它们能协作的基础。

接下来是本模块的核心 `UpdateConfig`：

[UpdateConfig 的流程注释（agent.go:78-87）](https://github.com/nginxinc/nginx-gateway-fabric/blob/5504d0d7de1706d1bda281281fbb0753a7b32806/internal/controller/nginx/agent/agent.go#L78-L87) —— 这段注释本身就是最好的「调用链说明书」，建议逐行读懂：设置文件 → 广播元数据 → Agent 收到 ConfigApplyRequest → Agent 反向 GetFile 拉每个文件 → Agent 更新 NGINX 并回 DataPlaneResponse → 订阅者回复 broadcaster 表示事务完成 → 错误写回 deployment。

[UpdateConfig 实现（agent.go:88-105）](https://github.com/nginxinc/nginx-gateway-fabric/blob/5504d0d7de1706d1bda281281fbb0753a7b32806/internal/controller/nginx/agent/agent.go#L88-L105) —— 三步：`SetFiles`（算版本）、`Send`（广播）、`SetLatestConfigError`。`msg == nil` 时打印 "No changes" 并直接 return。

版本计算在 `Deployment` 里：

[SetFiles 与 rebuildFileOverviews（deployment.go:204-209 与 272-317）](https://github.com/nginxinc/nginx-gateway-fabric/blob/5504d0d7de1706d1bda281281fbb0753a7b32806/internal/controller/nginx/agent/deployment.go#L272-L317) —— `rebuildFileOverviews` 把 `[]File` 转成 `[]*pb.File`（只含 `FileMeta`，**不含内容**），再叠加一组 `Unmanaged: true` 的文件（静态文件如 `nginx.conf`、以及用户 volume mount 的文件，告诉 Agent「这些别碰」），最后 `filesHelper.GenerateConfigVersion` 算 hash。`d.configVersion == newConfigVersion` 时返回 `nil`，这就是「无变更不下发」的闸门。

再看上游是怎么调用 `UpdateConfig` 的（这是调用链的起点）：

[handler.go 在 FileLock 保护下调用 updateNginxConf（handler.go:318-320）](https://github.com/nginxinc/nginx-gateway-fabric/blob/5504d0d7de1706d1bda281281fbb0753a7b32806/internal/controller/nginx/agent/../handler.go#L318-L320) —— 注意这里**先 `deployment.FileLock.Lock()`** 再调用，这是整个 ConfigApply 事务的互斥点（4.3 会讲为什么）。

[updateNginxConf 调用 UpdateConfig（handler.go:878-891）](https://github.com/nginxinc/nginx-gateway-fabric/blob/5504d0d7de1706d1bda281281fbb0753a7b32806/internal/controller/nginx/agent/../handler.go#L878-L891) —— `generator.Generate(conf)` 产出文件，紧接着 `nginxUpdater.UpdateConfig`，Plus 模式下再追加 `UpdateUpstreamServers`。

#### 4.1.4 代码实践

**实践目标**：确认 `UpdateConfig` 的「无变更即跳过」行为，并定位三个关键步骤在源码里的精确位置。

**操作步骤**：

1. 打开 [agent.go 的 UpdateConfig（agent.go:88-105）](https://github.com/nginxinc/nginx-gateway-fabric/blob/5504d0d7de1706d1bda281281fbb0753a7b32806/internal/controller/nginx/agent/agent.go#L88-L105)，在 `if msg == nil {` 这一行（第 94 行）和 `deployment.SetLatestConfigError(...)` 这一行（第 104 行）各加一行日志（**示例代码，仅为阅读练习，不要提交**）：
   ```go
   // 第 94 行后
   n.logger.V(1).Info("DEBUG: SetFiles returned nil msg, skipping broadcast")
   // 第 99 行前
   n.logger.V(1).Info("DEBUG: about to broadcast", "version", msg.ConfigVersion, "files", len(msg.FileOverviews))
   ```
2. 打开 [deployment.go 的 rebuildFileOverviews（deployment.go:303-316）](https://github.com/nginxinc/nginx-gateway-fabric/blob/5504d0d7de1706d1bda281281fbb0753a7b32806/internal/controller/nginx/agent/deployment.go#L303-L316)，确认 `configVersion` 比较那两行。

**需要观察的现象**：当你反复 apply 同一份 HTTPRoute（内容不变）时，由于 eventHandler 的 changed predicate（见 [u4-l4](u4-l4-change-processor-and-store.md)）通常已经在更上层过滤掉了，但万一到达 `UpdateConfig`，你会看到 `SetFiles returned nil msg` 的日志，而**不会**看到 `about to broadcast`。

**预期结果**：「无变更」路径走第 94-97 行早退；「有变更」路径走第 99 行广播。这条路径是否真的执行，取决于上游 predicate 是否放行，属正常。

**待本地验证**：实际日志输出需在 kind 集群里运行 NGF 才能观察到；纯阅读可跳过运行，只做源码定位。

#### 4.1.5 小练习与答案

**练习 1**：`UpdateConfig` 既不接收 context 也不返回 error，那「下发失败」是如何被上游知道的？

**参考答案**：错误不通过返回值传递，而是写进 `Deployment` 的状态——`deployment.SetLatestConfigError(deployment.GetConfigurationStatus())`。`GetConfigurationStatus`（见 [deployment.go:150-164](https://github.com/nginxinc/nginx-gateway-fabric/blob/5504d0d7de1706d1bda281281fbb0753a7b32806/internal/controller/nginx/agent/deployment.go#L150-L164)）把所有 Pod 的错误聚合成一个 error。eventHandler 在 `FileLock.Unlock()` 之后用 `deployment.GetLatestConfigError()` 取出，再 enqueue 成 status（见 [handler.go:322-335](https://github.com/nginxinc/nginx-gateway-fabric/blob/5504d0d7de1706d1bda281281fbb0753a7b32806/internal/controller/handler.go#L322-L335)）。

**练习 2**：`UpdateUpstreamServers` 开头有 `if !n.plus { return }`。为什么这个方法仍留在 `NginxUpdater` 接口里，而不是单独建一个 Plus 专属接口？

**参考答案**：为了调用方（eventHandler）的简单。eventHandler 只持有一个 `NginxUpdater`，在 [handler.go:887-890](https://github.com/nginxinc/nginx-gateway-fabric/blob/5504d0d7de1706d1bda281281fbb0753a7b32806/internal/controller/handler.go#L887-L890) 里无条件调用 `UpdateUpstreamServers`，由实现内部用 `n.plus` 判断是否真正执行。这样 OSS / Plus 走同一套调用代码，差异被封装在实现内部。
